name: Casanare (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the blood and valour of the Llaneros in the wars of independence
the vast llanos (plains) of Casanare and the hope of its people
the petroleum and other natural wealth of the department, represented by a central sun

description: A flag divided diagonally from the upper hoist to the lower fly: the upper-hoist triangle red, the lower-fly triangle green, with a yellow eight-pointed sun at the centre.
history: Casanare was created as an Intendancy and elevated to a department by the 1991 Constitution. Its flag combines the colours of the Llanos plains (green) and the patriot blood of the Llaneros (red), with a golden sun symbolising the petroleum wealth that drives the regional economy.

features.symbolism: diagonal division evoking the boundary between the Llanos plains (green) and the Andean piedmont (red), with a central golden sun
a golden sun standing for the petroleum wealth and the radiant warmth of the Llanos
